div.h1: Query Planner Cost Model: TableScan vs IndexScan

p.em: Technical analysis of crossover points and I/O overheads

div.p: The Query Planner switches to Table Scan when cost_idx > cost_scan.

blockquote.p: Observation: For this dataset size, the index is only efficient if returning fewer than 35 rows.

p.em: On spinning disks (HDD), the index crossover happens at even lower selectivity (e.g., 0.05%) due to the massive seek penalty.

ul.li: Mechanism: Reads every leaf page in the B-Tree or Heap.
Mechanism: Traverse B-Tree, scan range, then fetch heap rows.

div.p: Constraint: sel < 0.35% Use for Primary Key lookups or unique constraints. I/O Pattern: Sparse random access.
Constraint: sel ≈ 0.35% The "Tipping Point". Modern SSDs reduce R, potentially moving this to 1-2%. Planner Action: Prefer Scan if equal.
Constraint: sel > 0.35% Frequent in status columns (e.g., is_active). Why Scan? Avoids "Thrashing" the heap pages via random I/O.
